Event: Plants marijuana in Finn's locker to force him to join Glee
Explanation: He gets a talented singer for his club and faces no consequences while coercing and frightening a student.

Event: Tells Finn later that he planted the marijuana
Explanation: He risks Finn's view of him but gains honesty and Finn responds positively, strengthening their bond.

Event: Uses Glee Club to put on shows to impress Emma
Explanation: He leverages students’ work to pursue his romantic interest, benefiting himself more than them.

Event: Hits on Emma after promising Carl he wouldn't
Explanation: He chases his own romantic desires at the expense of Carl’s trust and relationship stability.

Event: Steals Finn's letterman jacket the first time
Explanation: He takes a sentimental object for himself, slightly comforting himself but clearly violating Finn.

Event: Lectures Puck for stealing Finn's jacket while secretly keeping it
Explanation: He postures as moral authority yet plans to keep the jacket, mildly easing his guilt while misleading others.

Event: Steals Finn's letterman jacket after Finn’s death and breaks down
Explanation: He harms himself emotionally and disrespects boundaries while gaining only painful catharsis that somewhat honors his grief.

Event: Drills Mercedes harshly at the start of season three
Explanation: His strictness is more about control than support, aiding his goals while discouraging her.

Event: Drills Marley harshly during Katy vs. Gaga week
Explanation: Again his harsh coaching prioritizes performance expectations over a student’s well-being.

Event: Fails to intervene when football players harass Kurt and throw him in a dumpster
Explanation: His inaction preserves his own comfort and avoids conflict while allowing serious bullying to proceed.

Event: Does nothing about repeated slushie attacks on Glee kids
Explanation: He keeps his own life easier by ignoring ongoing harassment that hurts his students.

Event: Does nothing when club members mock Rachel and threaten to punch her
Explanation: He avoids difficult discipline conversations while leaving Rachel unprotected.

Event: Confronts Terri about her fake pregnancy and tears off the fake baby bump
Explanation: He asserts himself and frees himself from deception, but does so in a frightening, aggressive way that harms her.

Event: Walks out on Terri and stops taking her “bullshit”
Explanation: Ending a toxic marriage greatly benefits him and removes an unhealthy dynamic affecting others.

Event: Blows up in fury when he finds out Terri was faking pregnancy
Explanation: His rage gives emotional release but damages communication and likely his own stability more than it helps.

Event: Risks his life to find Brittany during the school shooting incident
Explanation: He endangers himself significantly to protect Brittany, with little direct self-benefit.

Event: Unhangs Sue’s telephone after she steals gifts for homeless children
Explanation: He mildly inconveniences an antagonist with essentially no downside to himself and minor satisfaction.

Event: Invites Vocal Adrenaline to watch New Directions perform funk as “revenge” for Rachel’s treatment
Explanation: He channels anger into a performance that boosts his team’s confidence and teaches the rival group a lesson.

Event: Stops his Glee guys from directly retaliating against Vocal Adrenaline
Explanation: He protects his students from escalation and consequences while maintaining his authority and values.

Event: Sleeps on the free mattress, endangering Glee’s eligibility
Explanation: A trivial comfort almost costs the team Sectionals, with little meaningful gain for him.

Event: Takes the eligibility “bullet” so the team can still go to Sectionals
Explanation: He sacrifices his own ability to compete to ensure the students can go, strongly benefiting them at his expense.

Event: Acts as a father figure to Finn over time
Explanation: He gains meaningful relationship and purpose while giving Finn guidance and emotional support.

Event: Tells Finn he thought Finn was pathetic after leaving the military
Explanation: His cruel honesty damages Finn and their bond while providing him no clear benefit.

Event: Gives emotional “Journey” speech that it’s the journey that counts
Explanation: He motivates and reassures the students, strengthening their morale and his leadership.

Event: Gives rousing speech before Regionals, affirming belief and love in them
Explanation: He deepens their confidence and cohesion, and his own sense of purpose.

Event: Frequently looks out for students and goes out of his way for their interests
Explanation: Consistent mentorship helps the kids thrive and gives him fulfillment.

Event: Acts as “Team Mom,” tracking emotional well-being and holding group together
Explanation: He provides emotional labor that benefits the club while giving him identity and connection.

Event: Makes things about himself and does bad things if they benefit him
Explanation: His self-centered choices boost his ego or goals while often undermining others.

Event: Uses his “Chick Magnet” status in “Funk” to seduce Sue with dancing
Explanation: He gains leverage over Sue and some petty revenge while emotionally manipulating her.

Event: Seduce‑teases Sue in “Funk” despite her loathing him
Explanation: He enjoys power and advantage over an enemy while toying with her feelings.

Event: Lets April stay at his house while still married
Explanation: He gets companionship and excitement while destabilizing his marriage and confusing boundaries.

Event: Makes out with Shelby while married/entangled with others
Explanation: He satisfies his desires but complicates multiple relationships, risking later fallout.

Event: Cheats on Terri with Emma
Explanation: He gains illicit emotional/physical satisfaction but undermines his marriage and Emma’s position.

Event: Gets angry at Kurt and Mercedes for joining Cheerios despite others already on teams
Explanation: He clings to control and his vision for them while unfairly limiting their opportunities.

Event: Assigns “Funk” revenge plan and frames lesson so that “lesson learned: don’t go after his kids”
Explanation: He turns vengeance into a performance that empowers his students and deters bullies with minimal real harm.

Event: Lectures club in “Special Education” on their selfish behavior and his failings
Explanation: Honest criticism and self-critique help reset group dynamics, improving both his leadership and their behavior.

Event: Gets called out by Emma for “slutty” behavior in front of faculty (Emma’s action)
Explanation: She risks her own social comfort to hold him publicly accountable, which benefits the community more than herself.
